description: Fix the PluginMenu npm URL (currently uses short plugin name like 'github' instead of the full npm package name like '@opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-github') and fix the E2E test 'uninstall plugin via three-dot menu' that is timing out due to our recent PluginSearchResult displayName changes.

userStories.title: Add npmPackageName to ConfigStatePlugin and wire it through to the extension
userStories.description: The PluginMenu in the Chrome extension side panel generates an npm URL using plugin.name (e.g., 'github'), which produces https://www.npmjs.com/package/github — a wrong URL. The correct URL needs the full npm package name (e.g., '@opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-github'). Add an optional npmPackageName field to ConfigStatePlugin in shared types, populate it in serializePluginForExtension, and use it in PluginMenu to construct the correct npm URL.
acceptanceCriteria: ConfigStatePlugin in platform/shared/src/index.ts has an optional npmPackageName field of type string
serializePluginForExtension in platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts includes npmPackageName when the RegisteredPlugin has one
PluginMenu's VersionItem uses plugin.npmPackageName (falling back to plugin.name) when constructing the npm URL
The npm URL for an npm-sourced plugin like 'github' resolves to https://www.npmjs.com/package/@opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-github
Local plugins (no npmPackageName) still render the FolderOpen menu item correctly
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/shared/src/index.ts (add npmPackageName field to ConfigStatePlugin)
- platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts (include npmPackageName in serializePluginForExtension)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginMenu.tsx (fix VersionItem URL)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/bridge.ts (PluginState type inherits from ConfigStatePlugin so it auto-gets the field)

Context:
The RegisteredPlugin type in platform/mcp-server/src/state.ts already has `npmPackageName?: string` at line 111 — it stores the full npm package name (e.g., '@opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-github'). But serializePluginForExtension at line 144-200 in extension-handlers.ts does NOT include it in the wire format.

The PluginState type in bridge.ts is derived from ConfigStatePlugin: `type PluginState = Omit<ConfigStatePlugin, 'tools'> & { tools?: WireToolDef[]; sourcePath?: string };` — so adding npmPackageName to ConfigStatePlugin automatically makes it available in the extension.

Changes:
1. In platform/shared/src/index.ts, ConfigStatePlugin interface (line 307-336): add `npmPackageName?: string;` after `source: 'npm' | 'local';`

2. In platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts, serializePluginForExtension (line 175-199): add to the return object:
   `...(plugin.npmPackageName ? { npmPackageName: plugin.npmPackageName } : {}),`
   Also add `npmPackageName?: string;` to the return type annotation.

3. In platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginMenu.tsx, VersionItem (line 21-42): change line 24 from:
   `<Menu.Item onSelect={() => window.open(`https://www.npmjs.com/package/${plugin.name}`, '_blank')}>`
   to:
   `<Menu.Item onSelect={() => window.open(`https://www.npmjs.com/package/${plugin.npmPackageName ?? plugin.name}`, '_blank')}>`

Constraints:
- Do NOT rename or change plugin.name — it remains the short name used everywhere for tool prefixing and state management
- npmPackageName is optional — local plugins don't have one
- The PluginMenu.stories.tsx file may need updating if it tests the VersionItem rendering — check and update mock data to include npmPackageName

userStories.title: Fix E2E test 'uninstall plugin via three-dot menu' timeout
userStories.description: The E2E test at e2e/side-panel-npm-search.e2e.ts:147 ('uninstall plugin via three-dot menu and confirmation dialog') is timing out at 32.8s. This test pre-installs @opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-slack via npm install -g, starts the server with npm auto-discovery, and expects a 'Slack' card to appear in the side panel. Investigate and fix the timeout — the test may need adjustment for timing or the npm uninstall may be slow.
acceptanceCriteria: The E2E test 'uninstall plugin via three-dot menu and confirmation dialog' passes consistently
The test timeout is adequate for the npm install/uninstall operations it performs
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/side-panel-npm-search.e2e.ts (the failing test)

Context:
The test at line 147-227 does:
1. Pre-installs @opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-slack via `npm install -g` (line 156)
2. Starts MCP server with npm auto-discovery enabled (OPENTABS_SKIP_NPM_DISCOVERY: undefined)
3. Opens side panel and waits for 'Slack' card to appear (line 186-187)
4. Clicks three-dot menu > Uninstall > confirms in dialog
5. Waits for Slack card to disappear (line 210, 60s timeout)
6. Verifies via MCP that slack_ tools are gone

The test uses `test.slow()` which doubles the default timeout. The test was timing out at 32.8s.

Possible causes to investigate:
1. The npm uninstall triggered by the side panel may be slow in the isolated NPM_CONFIG_PREFIX environment
2. The waitForExtensionConnected or plugin discovery may be slow
3. The `slackTrigger.toBeHidden({ timeout: 60_000 })` wait at line 210 may not be matching correctly
4. The test may be hitting the npm search debounce or some other race condition

Run the test in isolation to reproduce: `npx playwright test e2e/side-panel-npm-search.e2e.ts -g 'uninstall'`

Constraints:
- Do not weaken the test assertions — the test should still verify the full uninstall flow
- The test already has `test.slow()` — adding more timeout may be acceptable if the npm operations genuinely need it
- Check if the test failure is consistent or flaky by running it multiple times
